---
title: "State Maps, scatter plots, and OLS"
format: html
---

# 1 Create maps 
## mapping policies (cep rate and NSLP spending vs child well-being variables)

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import geopandas as gpd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from pathlib import Path
from matplotlib.cm import ScalarMappable
from matplotlib.colors import Normalize
import os

# -----------------------
# Paths
# -----------------------
MASTER_DIR = Path(r"..\data\derived-data")
SHP_DIR    = Path(r"..\data\raw-data\shp_file\state")

MASTER_PATH = MASTER_DIR / "master_state_with_controls.csv"
SHP_PATH    = SHP_DIR / "cb_2018_us_state_500k.shp"

# -----------------------
# Columns
# -----------------------
COL_CEP   = "cep_rate_school"
COL_DOL   = "nslp_total_dollars_per_school_age"
COL_POV   = "poverty_rate_children"
COL_INSEC = "child_food_insec_rate"
COL_INSUF = "food_insufficiency_weighted"

# -----------------------
# Load + merge
# -----------------------
master = pd.read_csv(MASTER_PATH)
master["state_name"] = master["state_name"].astype(str).str.strip().str.replace(r"\s+", " ", regex=True)

states = gpd.read_file(SHP_PATH)
states["state_name"] = states["NAME"].astype(str).str.strip().str.replace(r"\s+", " ", regex=True)

gdf = states.merge(master, on="state_name", how="left")

# Keep 50 + DC
keep = [
    "Alabama","Alaska","Arizona","Arkansas","California","Colorado","Connecticut","Delaware",
    "District of Columbia","Florida","Georgia","Hawaii","Idaho","Illinois","Indiana","Iowa",
    "Kansas","Kentucky","Louisiana","Maine","Maryland","Massachusetts","Michigan","Minnesota",
    "Mississippi","Missouri","Montana","Nebraska","Nevada","New Hampshire","New Jersey",
    "New Mexico","New York","North Carolina","North Dakota","Ohio","Oklahoma","Oregon",
    "Pennsylvania","Rhode Island","South Carolina","South Dakota","Tennessee","Texas","Utah",
    "Vermont","Virginia","Washington","West Virginia","Wisconsin","Wyoming"
]
gdf = gdf[gdf["state_name"].isin(keep)].copy()

# Make numeric
for c in [COL_CEP, COL_DOL, COL_POV, COL_INSEC, COL_INSUF]:
    gdf[c] = pd.to_numeric(gdf[c], errors="coerce")
# Divide child poverty rate by 100
gdf[COL_POV] = gdf[COL_POV] / 100

# -----------------------
# IMPORTANT: CONUS only => big maps
# -----------------------
gdf_conus = gdf[~gdf["state_name"].isin(["Alaska", "Hawaii"])].copy()

# Label source: prefer STUSPS if exists, else state_name
label_col = "STUSPS" if "STUSPS" in gdf_conus.columns else "state_name"

# ------------------------
# Set the place where outputs are stored
# ------------------------
OUTPUT_DIR = "../figures/maps"
os.makedirs(OUTPUT_DIR, exist_ok=True)

# -----------------------
# Binning + plotting helper (no big legend box)
# -----------------------
k = 5  # quantile bins

def add_state_labels(ax, g):
    # representative_point is safer than centroid (keeps point inside polygon)
    pts = g.geometry.representative_point()
    for x, y, lab in zip(pts.x, pts.y, g[label_col].astype(str)):
        ax.text(x, y, lab, fontsize=6, ha="center", va="center")

def plot_pair_big(left_col, right_col, suptitle, left_title, right_title, left_cmap, right_cmap):
    # Quantile bins for each variable (intervals used as colorbar ticklabels)
    left_bin = pd.qcut(gdf_conus[left_col], q=k, duplicates="drop")
    right_bin = pd.qcut(gdf_conus[right_col], q=k, duplicates="drop")

    left_codes = left_bin.cat.codes.replace(-1, np.nan)
    right_codes = right_bin.cat.codes.replace(-1, np.nan)

    # Interval labels
    left_labels = [
        f"{iv.left:.1f}–{iv.right:.1f}"
        for iv in left_bin.cat.categories 
    ]
    right_labels = [
        f"{iv.left:.1f}–{iv.right:.1f}"
        for iv in right_bin.cat.categories
    ]

    # Use code range for colormap
    left_norm = Normalize(vmin=0, vmax=len(left_labels)-1)
    right_norm = Normalize(vmin=0, vmax=len(right_labels)-1)

    fig, axs = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(22, 10), constrained_layout=True)

    # ----- LEFT MAP
    tmp = gdf_conus.copy()
    tmp["_z"] = left_codes
    tmp.plot(
        column="_z", ax=axs[0], cmap=left_cmap, norm=left_norm,
        edgecolor="white", linewidth=0.5,
        missing_kwds={"color":"lightgrey"}
    )
    axs[0].set_title(left_title, fontsize=14)
    axs[0].set_axis_off()
    add_state_labels(axs[0], gdf_conus)

    sm_left = ScalarMappable(norm=left_norm, cmap=left_cmap)
    sm_left.set_array([])
    cbar_left = fig.colorbar(sm_left, ax=axs[0], fraction=0.035, pad=0.01)
    cbar_left.set_ticks(range(len(left_labels)))
    cbar_left.set_ticklabels(left_labels)
    cbar_left.ax.tick_params(labelsize=8)
    cbar_left.set_label(f"{left_col} (quantile bins)", fontsize=12)

    # ----- RIGHT MAP
    tmp = gdf_conus.copy()
    tmp["_z"] = right_codes
    tmp.plot(
        column="_z", ax=axs[1], cmap=right_cmap, norm=right_norm,
        edgecolor="white", linewidth=0.5,
        missing_kwds={"color":"lightgrey"}
    )
    axs[1].set_title(right_title, fontsize=14)
    axs[1].set_axis_off()
    add_state_labels(axs[1], gdf_conus)

    sm_right = ScalarMappable(norm=right_norm, cmap=right_cmap)
    sm_right.set_array([])
    cbar_right = fig.colorbar(sm_right, ax=axs[1], fraction=0.035, pad=0.01)
    cbar_right.set_ticks(range(len(right_labels)))
    cbar_right.set_ticklabels(right_labels)
    cbar_right.ax.tick_params(labelsize=6)
    cbar_right.set_label(f"{right_col} (quantile bins)", fontsize=12)

    fig.suptitle(suptitle, fontsize=18)


    # create file names
    filename = suptitle.replace(" ", "_").replace("(", "").replace(")", "") + ".png"

    # save the output
    path = os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, filename)
    plt.savefig(path, dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")

    plt.show()

# -----------------------
# 4 big pair-figures
# -----------------------
plot_pair_big(COL_CEP, COL_POV,
              "CEP vs Child Poverty (CONUS, big maps + labels)",
              "CEP participation rate (%)", "Child poverty rate (%)",
              "Blues", "Reds")

plot_pair_big(COL_CEP, COL_INSEC,
              "CEP vs Child Food Insecurity (CONUS, big maps + labels)",
              "CEP participation rate", "Child food insecurity rate",
              "Blues", "Reds")

plot_pair_big(COL_CEP, COL_INSUF,
              "CEP vs Child Food Insufficiency (CONUS, big maps + labels)",
              "CEP participation rate", "Child food insecurity rate",
              "Blues", "Reds")

plot_pair_big(COL_DOL, COL_POV,
              "NSLP $ per School-Age vs Child Poverty (CONUS, big maps + labels)",
              "NSLP $ per school-age", "Child poverty rate",
              "Purples", "Reds")

plot_pair_big(COL_DOL, COL_INSEC,
              "NSLP $ per School-Age vs Child Food Insecurity (CONUS, big maps + labels)",
              "NSLP $ per school-age", "Child food insecurity rate",
              "Purples", "Reds")

plot_pair_big(COL_DOL, COL_INSUF,
              "NSLP $ per School-Age vs Child Food Insufficiency (CONUS, big maps + labels)",
              "NSLP $ per school-age", "Child food insecurity rate",
              "Purples", "Reds")
```

## voting maps
```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import geopandas as gpd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from pathlib import Path
from matplotlib.cm import ScalarMappable
from matplotlib.colors import Normalize
from matplotlib.colors import TwoSlopeNorm
# -----------------------
# Paths
# -----------------------
MASTER_DIR = Path(r"C:\Users\gnolu\final project data vz\date cleaning\cleaning")
SHP_DIR    = Path(r"C:\Users\gnolu\final project data vz\date cleaning\cleaning\shp file\state")

MASTER_PATH = MASTER_DIR / "vote_state_2024_with_other_major_varis.csv"
SHP_PATH    = SHP_DIR / "cb_2018_us_state_500k.shp"

# -----------------------
# Columns
# -----------------------
COL_CEP   = "cep_rate_school"
COL_DOL   = "nslp_total_dollars_per_school_age"
COL_POV   = "poverty_rate_children"
COL_INSEC = "child_food_insec_rate"
COL_INSUF = "food_insufficiency_weighted"
COL_VOTE = "dem_margin"

# -----------------------
# Load + merge
# -----------------------
df = pd.read_csv(MASTER_PATH)
df["state_po"] = df["state_po"].astype(str).str.strip()
df["poverty_rate_children"] = df["poverty_rate_children"] / 100

states = gpd.read_file(SHP_PATH)
# Keep state abbreviation directly from shapefile
states = states[["STUSPS", "NAME", "geometry"]]
states = states.rename(columns={"STUSPS": "state_po"})

gdf = states.merge(df, on="state_po", how="left")

# -----------------------
# IMPORTANT: CONUS only => big maps
# -----------------------
gdf_conus = gdf[~gdf["state_po"].isin(["AK", "HI"])].copy()

# Explicit list: 48 contiguous states + DC
conus_keep = [
    "AL","AZ","AR","CA","CO","CT","DE","DC","FL","GA","ID","IL","IN","IA","KS","KY",
    "LA","ME","MD","MA","MI","MN","MS","MO","MT","NE","NV","NH","NJ","NM","NY","NC",
    "ND","OH","OK","OR","PA","RI","SC","SD","TN","TX","UT","VT","VA","WA","WV","WI","WY"
]
gdf_conus = gdf[gdf["state_po"].isin(conus_keep)].copy()

# Label source: prefer STUSPS if exists, else state_name
label_col = "state_po" 

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Plot a diverging choropleth for dem_margin (blue=Dem, red=GOP)
# Requirements:
# - gdf_conus: GeoDataFrame with geometry and a numeric "dem_margin" column
# - add_state_labels(ax, gdf_conus): your helper to add state labels
# - label_col: your label column name ("STUSPS" or "state_name")
# ------------------------------------------------------------
def add_state_labels(ax, g):
    # representative_point is safer than centroid (keeps point inside polygon)
    pts = g.geometry.representative_point()
    for x, y, lab in zip(pts.x, pts.y, g[label_col].astype(str)):
        ax.text(x, y, lab, fontsize=11, ha="center", va="center")

def plot_dem_margin_big(
    gdf_conus,
    dem_col="dem_margin",
    title="County/State-level Democratic margin (blue=Dem, red=GOP)",
    label_states=True
):
    # Make a copy to avoid mutating upstream objects
    g = gdf_conus.copy()

    # Ensure numeric (safe even if already numeric)
    g[dem_col] = pd.to_numeric(g[dem_col], errors="coerce")

    # Use symmetric bounds around zero so colors are comparable
    v = np.nanmax(np.abs(g[dem_col].values))
    vmin, vmax = -v, v

    # Center the color scale at 0
    norm = TwoSlopeNorm(vmin=vmin, vcenter=0.0, vmax=vmax)

    # Choose a diverging colormap where positive values are BLUE
    # - "RdBu_r" => positive (high) is blue, negative (low) is red
    cmap = plt.get_cmap("RdBu")

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(1, 1, figsize=(22, 10), constrained_layout=True)

    # Plot polygons
    g.plot(
        column=dem_col,
        ax=ax,
        cmap=cmap,
        norm=norm,
        edgecolor="white",
        linewidth=0.5,
        missing_kwds={"color": "lightgrey"}
    )

    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=18)
    ax.set_axis_off()

   
    add_state_labels(ax, g)

    # Add colorbar
    sm = ScalarMappable(norm=norm, cmap=cmap)
    sm.set_array([])

    cbar = fig.colorbar(sm, ax=ax, fraction=0.035, pad=0.01)
    cbar.set_label("Democratic margin (Dem share − GOP share)", fontsize=18)

    # Optional: nicer ticks (you can tweak)
    cbar.ax.tick_params(labelsize=16)

    plt.show()

def plot_dem_vs_cep_pair(
    gdf_conus,
    dem_col="dem_margin",
    cep_col="cep_rate_school",
    title="Democratic margin vs CEP participation rate (CONUS)",
    label_col="state_po",
    label_states=True,
    label_fontsize=11
):
    g = gdf_conus.copy()

    # Ensure numeric columns
    g[dem_col] = pd.to_numeric(g[dem_col], errors="coerce")
    g[cep_col] = pd.to_numeric(g[cep_col], errors="coerce")

    # --- Left map: Dem margin (diverging around 0)
    v = np.nanmax(np.abs(g[dem_col].values))
    dem_norm = TwoSlopeNorm(vmin=-v, vcenter=0.0, vmax=v)
    dem_cmap = plt.get_cmap("RdBu")  # positive -> blue, negative -> red

    # --- Right map: CEP rate (sequential)
    # If cep rate is 0-1, this works well. If it is 0-100, you may want to /100 first.
    cep_norm = Normalize(vmin=np.nanmin(g[cep_col]), vmax=np.nanmax(g[cep_col]))
    cep_cmap = plt.get_cmap("Blues")

    # Create the two-panel figure
    fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(24, 10), constrained_layout=True)

    # ---- LEFT: Dem margin
    g.plot(
        column=dem_col,
        ax=axes[0],
        cmap=dem_cmap,
        norm=dem_norm,
        edgecolor="white",
        linewidth=0.5,
        missing_kwds={"color": "lightgrey"}
    )
    axes[0].set_title("Democratic margin (Blue=Dem, Red=Rep)", fontsize=16)
    axes[0].set_axis_off()
    add_state_labels(axes[0], g)

    # ---- RIGHT: CEP rate
    g.plot(
        column=cep_col,
        ax=axes[1],
        cmap=cep_cmap,
        norm=cep_norm,
        edgecolor="white",
        linewidth=0.5,
        missing_kwds={"color": "lightgrey"}
    )
    axes[1].set_title("CEP participation rate", fontsize=16)
    axes[1].set_axis_off()

    # Tight zoom to CONUS bounds (removes excess whitespace)
    xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax = g.total_bounds
    for ax in axes:
        ax.set_xlim(xmin, xmax)
        ax.set_ylim(ymin, ymax)

    add_state_labels(axes[1], g)

    # Colorbars (one for each map)
    sm_dem = ScalarMappable(norm=dem_norm, cmap=dem_cmap)
    sm_dem.set_array([])
    cbar_dem = fig.colorbar(sm_dem, ax=axes[0], fraction=0.04, pad=0.01)
    cbar_dem.set_label("Dem margin (Dem share − Rep share)", fontsize=14)
    cbar_dem.ax.tick_params(labelsize=12)

    sm_cep = ScalarMappable(norm=cep_norm, cmap=cep_cmap)
    sm_cep.set_array([])
    cbar_cep = fig.colorbar(sm_cep, ax=axes[1], fraction=0.04, pad=0.01)
    cbar_cep.set_label("CEP rate", fontsize=14)
    cbar_cep.ax.tick_params(labelsize=12)
    fig.suptitle(title, fontsize=18)

    # create file names
    filename = title.replace(" ", "_").replace("(", "").replace(")", "") + ".png"

    # save the output
    path = os.path.join(OUTPUT_DIR, filename)
    plt.savefig(path, dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")

    plt.show()

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Call the function
# ------------------------------------------------------------
plot_dem_vs_cep_pair(
    gdf_conus,
    dem_col="dem_margin",
    cep_col="cep_rate_school",
    title="Political leaning vs CEP participation (CONUS)",
    label_col="state_po",
    label_states=True,
    label_fontsize=11
)
```

# Create scatter plots
## cep rate and NSLP spending vs child well-being variables
```{python}
# Scatter plot and fitted line
from re import X
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

df = pd.read_csv(r"..\data\derived-data\master_state_with_controls.csv")
df["poverty_rate_children"] = df["poverty_rate_children"] / 100

# use sns
def scatter_with_fit_sns(
    df, x, y, state_col="state_name",
    title=None, xlabel=None, ylabel=None,
    annotate=False, figsize=(7.2, 5.0),
    ci=95, point_alpha=0.7
):
    # drop missing
    d = df[[x, y, state_col]].dropna(subset=[x, y]).copy()

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=figsize)

    # scatter + fitted line (OLS)
    sns.regplot(
        data=d, x=x, y=y,
        ax=ax,
        ci=ci,                  # confidence interval band (set None to remove)
        scatter_kws={"alpha": point_alpha},
        line_kws={}
    )
    # --- correlation ---
    r = d[[x, y]].corr().iloc[0, 1]

    ax.text(
        0.02, 0.98,
        f"r = {r:.3f}",
        transform=ax.transAxes,
        ha="left", va="top",
        fontsize=12
    )

    ax.set_title(title if title else f"{y} vs {x}")
    ax.set_xlabel(xlabel if xlabel else x.replace("_", " ").title())
    ax.set_ylabel(ylabel if ylabel else y.replace("_", " ").title())

    # create file names
    filename = title.replace(" ", "_").replace("(", "").replace(")", "") + ".png"

    # save the output
    path = os.path.join("../figures/scatterplots/", filename)
    plt.savefig(path, dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")


    fig.tight_layout()
    return fig, ax

# ---- 4 key plots (Need on X, Policy on Y) ----
c1 = scatter_with_fit_sns(
    df,
    x="cep_rate_school",
    y="poverty_rate_children",
    title="Child poverty vs School CEP rate"
)

c2 = scatter_with_fit_sns(
    df,
    x="cep_rate_school",
    y="child_food_insec_rate",
    title="Food insecurity vs School CEP rate"
)

c3 = scatter_with_fit_sns(
    df,
    x="nslp_total_dollars_per_school_age",
    y="poverty_rate_children",
    title="Child poverty vs NSLP dollars per school-age child"
)

c4 = scatter_with_fit_sns(
    df,
    x="nslp_total_dollars_per_school_age",
    y="child_food_insec_rate",
    title="Food insecurity vs NSLP dollars per school-age child"
)

c5 = scatter_with_fit_sns(
    df,
    x="nslp_total_dollars_per_school_age",
    y="food_insufficiency_weighted",
    title="Food insufficiency vs NSLP dollars per school-age child"
)

c6 = scatter_with_fit_sns(
    df,
    x="cep_rate_school",
    y="food_insufficiency_weighted",
    title="Food insufficiency vs School CEP rate"
)
c1
c2
c3
c4
c5
c6

```

## voting
```{python}
import pandas as pd
# Show the correlationships
correlation_matrix = gdf_conus[
    ["dem_margin", "cep_rate_school", "poverty_rate_children"]
].corr()

print(correlation_matrix)

# show the scatter plot
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

sns.regplot(
    data=gdf_conus,
    x="dem_margin",
    y="cep_rate_school",
    scatter_kws={"alpha":0.3},
    line_kws={"color":"red"}
)
# correlation
x = "dem_margin"
y = "cep_rate_school"
r = gdf_conus[[x, y]].dropna().corr().iloc[0, 1]
plt.text(
    0.02, 0.98,
    f"r = {r:.3f}",
    transform=plt.gca().transAxes,
    ha="left", va="top",
    fontsize=12
)
plt.ylim(0, 1.19)
plt.title("Political Leaning vs CEP Participation (CONUS)")
plt.xlabel("Democratic Margin")
plt.ylabel("CEP School Participation Rate")

plt.savefig("../figures/scatterplots/dem_margin_vs_cep.png", dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")

plt.show()

sns.regplot(
    data=gdf_conus,
    x="dem_margin",
    y="poverty_rate_children",
    scatter_kws={"alpha":0.3},
    line_kws={"color":"red"}
)
# correlation
x = "dem_margin"
y = "poverty_rate_children"
r = gdf_conus[[x, y]].dropna().corr().iloc[0, 1]
plt.text(
    0.02, 0.98,
    f"r = {r:.3f}",
    transform=plt.gca().transAxes,
    ha="left", va="top",
    fontsize=12
)

plt.title("Political Leaning vs Child Poverty Rate (CONUS)")
plt.xlabel("Democratic Margin")
plt.ylabel("Child Poverty Rate")
plt.savefig("../figures/scatterplots/dem_margin_vs_child_pov.png", dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")

plt.show()
```

# Create regression (regressing CEP on child pov and dem margin) 
```{python}
# ------------------------------------
# Residual analysis
# ------------------------------------
import statsmodels.api as sm
import matplotlib.patheffects as pe
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Compute poverty-controlled CEP = residuals from:
#    CEP ~ poverty
# ------------------------------------------------------------
g = gdf_conus.copy()

# Ensure numeric
g["cep_rate_school"] = pd.to_numeric(g["cep_rate_school"], errors="coerce")
g["poverty_rate_children"] = pd.to_numeric(g["poverty_rate_children"], errors="coerce")

# Drop missing rows for regression
reg_df = g[["cep_rate_school", "poverty_rate_children"]].dropna().copy()

# OLS: CEP ~ poverty
X = sm.add_constant(reg_df["poverty_rate_children"])
y = reg_df["cep_rate_school"]
model = sm.OLS(y, X).fit()

# Put residuals back into g (aligned by index)
g["cep_resid"] = np.nan
g.loc[reg_df.index, "cep_resid"] = model.resid

print(model.summary())  # Optional: check coefficient + R^2

# -----
# multivariate regression (holding the other variable constant)
# -----
import statsmodels.api as sm_ols
# Drop missing rows
reg_df = g[["cep_rate_school", "poverty_rate_children", "dem_margin"]].dropna().copy()

# Define X and y
X = reg_df[["poverty_rate_children", "dem_margin"]]
X = sm_ols.add_constant(X)
y = reg_df["cep_rate_school"]

# Run OLS
model2 = sm_ols.OLS(y, X).fit()

print(model2.summary())

res = model2  

coef_tbl = pd.DataFrame({
    "coef": res.params,
    "std_err": res.bse,
    "t": res.tvalues,
    "p_value": res.pvalues,
    "ci_low": res.conf_int()[0],
    "ci_high": res.conf_int()[1],
})

# Summarize the result
coef_tbl = coef_tbl.round({"coef": 3, "std_err": 3, "t": 3, "p_value": 3, "ci_low": 3, "ci_high": 3})
```

```{python}
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv("../data/raw-data/file.csv")
df.head()
```

```{python}
import pandas as pd

df = pd.read_csv("../data/raw-data/file.csv")
df.head()
```
